title: An acoustic treatment method and device
abstract: A method of culturing a microorganism, the method including: (i) providing a solution containing a microorganism; (ii) acoustically treating the solution with sound waves having a sine waveform at a preselected frequency and at a power intensity of less than 10 W/cm 2 to produce a minimum 10% increase in microorganism concentration compared to a non- acoustically treated solution for a same treatment time, wherein the solution is acoustically treated in a vessel having a volume of at least 12.5L; and (iii) optionally isolating the acoustically-treated microorganism.
claims:
Claims 1. A method of culturing a microorganism, the method including : (i) providing a solution containing a microorganism; (ii) acoustically treating the solution with sound waves having a sine waveform at a preselected frequency and at a power intensity of less than 10 W/cm 2 to produce a minimum 10% increase in microorganism concentration compared to a non-acoustically treated solution for a same treatment time, wherein the solution is acoustically treated in a vessel having a volume of at least 12.5L; and (iii) optionally isolating the acoustically-treated microorganism. 2. The method according to claim 1, wherein step (ii) includes subjecting the solution to sound waves at a frequency not exceeding 2,000 Hz or at least 750,000 Hz. 3. The method according to either claim 1 or 2, including subjecting the solution to sound waves at a resonant frequency of the solution. 4. The method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein step (ii) includes a treatment time of up to 10 days . 5. The method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the method includes maintaining the solution at a temperature not exceeding 50Â°C. 6. The method according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein step (iii) includes subjecting the acoustically- treated microorganism to solid-liquid separation. . The method according to any one of the preceding claims including transmitting sound waves at a dB Sound Pressure Level (SPL) not exceeding 150 dB . 8. The method according to any one of the preceding claims, including transmitting sound waves having a duty cycle of at least 50%. 9. The method according to any one of the preceding claims, including transmitting sound waves having an RMS power of at least 5 W. 10. A method of producing a yeast-fermented food product according to the invention may comprise: (i) providing a precursor of the food product; (ii) inoculating the precursor with a microorganism to form an inoculate; (iii) acoustically treating the inoculate with sound waves having a sine waveform at a preselected frequency and at a power intensity of less than 10 W/cm 2 to produce a minimum 10% reduction in fermentation time compared to a non-acoustically-treated inoculate and form a fermentate, wherein the inoculate is acoustically treated in a vessel having a volume of at least 12.5L; and (iv) optionally removing the yeast from the fermentate and forming the fermented food product. 11. The method according to claim 10, wherein step (iii) includes subjecting the solution to sound waves at a frequency not exceeding 2,000 Hz or at least 750,000 Hz. 12. The method according to either claim 10 or 11, wherein step (i) includes maintaining a solution of the precursor 

 at a temperature not exceeding 80Â°C for at least 30 minutes . 13. The method according to any one of claims 10 to 12, wherein step (iii) includes a treatment time of up to 10 days . 14. The method according to any one of claims 10 to 13, including subjecting the inoculate to sound waves at a resonant frequency of the inoculate. 15. The method according to any one of claims 10 to 14, wherein step (iv) includes subjecting the fermentate to solid-liquid separation to isolate the microorganism. 16. The method according to any one of claims 10 to 15 including transmitting sound waves at a dB Sound Pressure Level (SPL) not exceeding 150 dB . 17. The method according to any one of claims 10 to 16, including transmitting sound waves having a duty cycle of at least 50%. 18. The method according to any one of claims 10 to 17, including transmitting sound waves having an RMS (power of at least 5 W. 19. An acoustic device comprising: a housing having a first end that is configured to receive a sound generator and a second end including a connector that is connectable to a fermenter or microorganism culturing tank having a volume of at least 12.5 L ; and a sound generator located within the housing configured to transmit sound waves having a sine waveform 

 at a preselected frequency and at a power intensity of less than 10 W/cm 2 into the fermenter or microbial culturing tank. 20. The acoustic device according to claim 19, wherein the acoustic device is configured to transmit sound waves at a frequency not exceeding 2,000 Hz or at least 750,000 Hz . 21. The acoustic device according to either claim 19 or 20, wherein the connector comprises a cap comprising a domeshaped top with a circumferential rim. 22. The acoustic device according to any one of claims 19 to 21, wherein the housing has a f rustoconical body.
